heading: Imposition of sanctions with respect to the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran
label: 8803 Imposition of sanctions with respect to the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran
notes: Delegation of FunctionsFor delegation of certain functions and authorities vested in the President by this section, see Memorandum of President of the United States, , 78 F.R. 35545, set out as a note under .June 3, 2013section 8801 of this title

children.text: Iran’s energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors and Iran’s ports are facilitating the Government of Iran’s nuclear proliferation activities by providing revenue to support proliferation activities.
The United Nations Security Council and the United States Government have expressed concern about the proliferation risks presented by the Iranian nuclear program.

The Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency (in this section referred to as the “IAEA”) has in successive reports (GOV/2012/37 and GOV/2011/65) identified possible military dimensions of Iran’s nuclear program.

The Government of Iran continues to defy the requirements and obligations contained in relevant IAEA Board of Governors and United Nations Security Council resolutions, including by continuing and expanding uranium enrichment activities in Iran, as reported in IAEA Report GOV/2012/37.

United Nations Security Council Resolution 1929 (2010) recognizes the “potential connection between Iran’s revenues derived from its energy sector and the funding of Iran’s proliferation sensitive nuclear activities”.

The National Iranian Tanker Company is the main carrier for the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps-designated National Iranian Oil Company and a key element in the petroleum supply chain responsible for generating energy revenues that support the illicit nuclear proliferation activities of the Government of Iran.

children.heading: Designation of ports and entities in the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran as entities of proliferation concern
children.label: b Designation of ports and entities in the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran as entities of proliferation concern
children.text: Entities that operate ports in Iran and entities in the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran, including the National Iranian Oil Company, the National Iranian Tanker Company, the Islamic Republic of Iran Shipping Lines, and their affiliates, play an important role in Iran’s nuclear proliferation efforts and all such entities are hereby designated as entities of proliferation concern.

children.heading: Blocking of property of entities in energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors
children.label: c Blocking of property of entities in energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On and after the date that is 180 days after , the President shall block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property of any person described in paragraph (2) if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.January 2, 2013

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The requirement to block and prohibit all transactions in all property and interests in property under subparagraph (A) shall not include the authority to impose sanctions on the importation of goods.

children.label: A
children.text: is part of the energy, shipping, or shipbuilding sectors of Iran;

children.label: B
children.text: operates a port in Iran; or

children.label: i
children.text: a person determined under subparagraph (A) to be a part of the energy, shipping, or shipbuilding sectors of Iran;

children.label: ii
children.text: a person determined under subparagraph (B) to operate a port in Iran; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an Iranian person included on the list of specially designated nationals and blocked persons maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the Department of the Treasury (other than an Iranian financial institution described in paragraph (3)).

children.label: A
children.text: Iran’s proliferation of weapons of mass destruction or delivery systems for weapons of mass destruction;

children.label: B
children.text: Iran’s support for international terrorism; or

children.label: C
children.text: Iran’s abuses of human rights.

children.heading: Additional sanctions with respect to the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran
children.label: d Additional sanctions with respect to the energy, shipping, and shipbuilding sectors of Iran

children.heading: Sale, supply, or transfer of certain goods and services
children.label: 1 Sale, supply, or transfer of certain goods and services

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in this section, the President shall impose 5 or more of the sanctions described in section 6(a) of the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 (; note) with respect to a person if the President determines that the person knowingly, on or after the date that is 180 days after , sells, supplies, or transfers to or from Iran goods or services described in paragraph (3).Public Law 104–17250 U.S.C. 1701January 2, 2013

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The requirement to impose sanctions under subparagraph (A) shall not include the authority to impose sanctions relating to the importation of goods under paragraph (8)(A) or (12) of section 6(a) of the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996, and any sanction relating to the importation of goods shall not count for purposes of the requirement to impose sanctions under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Facilitation of certain transactions
children.label: 2 Facilitation of certain transactions
children.text: Except as provided in this section, the President shall prohibit the opening, and prohibit or impose strict conditions on the maintaining, in the United States of a correspondent account or a payable-through account by a foreign financial institution that the President determines knowingly, on or after the date that is 180 days after , conducts or facilitates a significant financial transaction for the sale, supply, or transfer to or from Iran of goods or services described in paragraph (3).January 2, 2013

children.heading: Goods and services described
children.label: 3 Goods and services described
children.text: Goods or services described in this paragraph are significant goods or services used in connection with the energy, shipping, or shipbuilding sectors of Iran, including the National Iranian Oil Company, the National Iranian Tanker Company, and the Islamic Republic of Iran Shipping Lines.

children.heading: Humanitarian exception
children.label: e Humanitarian exception
children.text: The President may not impose sanctions under this section with respect to any person for conducting or facilitating a transaction for the sale of agricultural commodities, food, medicine, or medical devices to Iran or for the provision of humanitarian assistance to the people of Iran.
to the extent that the President determines that such an exception is in the national interest of the United States; and
if the President submits to the appropriate congressional committees a notification of and justification for the exception not later than 15 days before issuing the exception.

children.heading: Applicability of sanctions to petroleum and petroleum products
children.label: g Applicability of sanctions to petroleum and petroleum products

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), this section shall apply with respect to the purchase of petroleum or petroleum products from Iran only if, at the time of the purchase, a determination of the President under that the price and supply of petroleum and petroleum products produced in countries other than Iran is sufficient to permit purchasers of petroleum and petroleum products from Iran to reduce significantly their purchases from Iran is in effect.section 8513a(d)(4)(B) of this title

children.heading: Exportation
children.label: A Exportation
children.text: This section shall not apply with respect to the exportation of petroleum or petroleum products from Iran to a country to which the exception under applies at the time of the exportation of the petroleum or petroleum products.section 8513a(d)(4)(D)(i) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: This section shall not apply with respect to a financial transaction described in clause (ii) conducted or facilitated by a foreign financial institution if, at the time of the transaction, the exception under applies to the country with primary jurisdiction over the foreign financial institution.section 8513a(d)(4)(D)(i) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: not otherwise subject to sanctions under the law of the United States; and

children.label: bb
children.text: between the country with primary jurisdiction over the foreign financial institution and Iran; and

children.label: II
children.text: any funds owed to Iran as a result of such trade are credited to an account located in the country with primary jurisdiction over the foreign financial institution.

children.heading: Applicability of sanctions to natural gas
children.label: h Applicability of sanctions to natural gas

children.heading: Sale, supply, or transfer
children.label: 1 Sale, supply, or transfer
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), this section shall not apply to the sale, supply, or transfer to or from Iran of natural gas.

children.label: i
children.text: not otherwise subject to sanctions under the law of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: between the country with primary jurisdiction over the foreign financial institution and Iran; and

children.label: B
children.text: any funds owed to Iran as a result of such trade are credited to an account located in the country with primary jurisdiction over the foreign financial institution.

children.label: A
children.text: determines that such a waiver is vital to the national security of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: submits to the appropriate congressional committees a report providing a justification for the waiver.

children.heading: Form of report
children.label: 2 Form of report
children.text: Each report submitted under paragraph (1)(B) shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified annex.
